== Notes for [[Item:Q106671|Q106671]] ==

Progenitor of ''Fatimid Imamate'' ('''Caliphate''') of '''Egypt'''.

'''7th Shia Ismai'li Imaam'''

* Date of Ascension: c. 137 AH or 158 AH (c. 755 AD or 775 AD)
* Period of Imamate: 45 Years
* Wilaadat (Birth): 12th Rabi ul-Awwal 128 AH (14th December 745 AD)
* Wafaat (Death): 11th Shawwal 193 AH (31st July 809 AD)

The group that believed Muhammad ibn Ismail to be the Mahdi who had withdrawn into occultation and would return again to earth some day, came to be known as the Seveners. This term is often incorrectly applied to the "Ismailis" who had separated from the Seveners and gone further on with the succession to the Imamat.

Source 1: Sahifa; Books of Da'wat-e-Haadiyah by Mulla Saadullah Husami

Source 2: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Muhammad_ibn_Ismail

Source 3: http://www.ismaili.net/histoire/history04/history408.html

Source 4: http://www.ismaili.net/histoire/history04/history409.html

Source 5: http://www.ismaili.net/histoire/history04/history410.html

Source 6: http://www.ismaili.net/histoire/history04/history411.html

Source 7: http://www.ismaili.net/histoire/history04/history412.html

Source 8: http://www.ismaili.net/histoire/history04/history413.html
--------------------
'''7th Fatimid Imaam'''

* ''Imaamate Period/ Reign: 34 years; 95-95 AH (714- AD)''
* Wilaadat (Birth):
* Wafaat (Death): 95 AH (714 AD)

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
geni:4796519627780042316

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
